signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long is still green, retaining about 91% of current peak unrealized PnL, and the live reduce-only stop near 2352 plus TP near 2458 remain on exchange, so this stays a bounded-risk management hold rather than a cleanup exit. The fresh VETO argues against rotating into anything new, not against continuing to manage a small already-protected winner.

BTC remains broadly constructive but the board is back under a real VETO tied to live conflict headlines, which cuts against any fresh action. That keeps it neutral rather than a better management option than the existing ETH hold.
SOL is still following the same broad tape and is not offering a cleaner profile than the current ETH long. Under VETO it stays watch-only.
AZTEC remains too fresh and execution-sensitive to justify rotating away from a small protected ETH winner. Better watched than traded here.
VVV is still too unstable and reactive to trust as a cleaner management alternative. The existing ETH position is simpler and better defined.
MAVIA can move sharply, but that volatility makes it a lower-quality management switch while the board is vetoed. Hold only.
TST remains a noisy momentum name after its squeeze-style move, which keeps fresh action low quality under the current VETO regime. Not a better use of risk than managing ETH.
STBL can still look mechanically interesting, but it remains unstable enough that it does not outrank the live ETH position. Watch-only under the event brake.
PROMPT is too thin and too reactive to trust under active conflict headlines. It stays out of the action set while ETH remains the cleaner managed exposure.
LDO still has some constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify leaving the existing ETH hold. Under VETO it stays neutral.
FARTCOIN remains active and green, but this is still a noisy squeeze continuation that becomes harder to manage under VETO. No rotation away from ETH.
